description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has just received a bug-report saying that the
    application is no longer writing DEBUG log lines even though it clearly should.
    Your job is to (1) correct the service’s logging configuration and (2) create a
    very small “sanity-check” summary file proving that DEBUG lines are now enabled.

    Current project layout (already present on disk):

      /home/user/devops-demo/
        ├── bin/
        │   └── run.sh
        ├── config/
        │   └── service.conf
        └── logs/
            └── app.log

    1. Configuration file to fix  
       • Path : /home/user/devops-demo/config/service.conf  
       • It is an INI file and presently looks like this (**do not change the section header**):
         [logging]
         log_level = INFO
         log_file  = ./logs/app.log

       You must change it so that it contains exactly the two key/value pairs below
       (order does not matter, spacing is free, but key names are case-sensitive):
         log_level = DEBUG
         log_file  = /home/user/devops-demo/logs/app.log

       No other keys should be added or removed.

    2. Create a DEBUG summary file  
       • Path  : /home/user/devops-demo/logs/debug_summary.log  
       • Purpose: Provide a one-glance count of how many DEBUG and ERROR lines are
                  **currently** in /home/user/devops-demo/logs/app.log.  
       • Required format (three literal lines, newline-terminated, no extra spaces):

            DEBUG LOG SUMMARY
            DEBUG=<integer>
            ERROR=<integer>

         Line-1 must be the fixed title “DEBUG LOG SUMMARY”.  
         Line-2 must show the total number of log lines in app.log that contain the
         substring “DEBUG”.  
         Line-3 must show the total number of log lines in app.log that contain the
         substring “ERROR”.

    3. Validation hints (what the automated checker will look for)
       • /home/user/devops-demo/config/service.conf must now have log_level=DEBUG and the
         absolute log_file path as written above.  
       • /home/user/devops-demo/logs/debug_summary.log must exist and must follow the
         exact three-line format shown, with correct counts.  
       • For the shipping log that exists right now, the correct counts are what you
         obtain by doing “grep -c DEBUG app.log” and “grep -c ERROR app.log”.

    4. Complexity guideline  
       The entire workflow can be completed with roughly 5-10 well-chosen terminal
       commands (editing the file, counting log lines, echoing the summary).

    Do not remove or rename any existing files; only edit the specified config file
    and create the single summary log file.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth objects used by the automated test suite -->

    <!-- Initial contents of /home/user/devops-demo/config/service.conf BEFORE the agent works -->
    <initial_service_conf>
[logging]
log_level = INFO
log_file  = ./logs/app.log
    </initial_service_conf>

    <!-- File present before task begins -->
    <initial_app_log>/home/user/devops-demo/logs/app.log</initial_app_log>

    <!-- Exact contents of app.log so that we can verify counts -->
    <initial_app_log_contents>
2023-07-01 12:00:00,001 - root - INFO  - Service started
2023-07-01 12:00:05,123 - root - DEBUG - Loaded config
2023-07-01 12:00:10,999 - root - ERROR - Connection failed
2023-07-01 12:00:15,456 - root - DEBUG - Retrying connection
2023-07-01 12:00:20,321 - root - DEBUG - Connection established
2023-07-01 12:00:25,888 - root - INFO  - Service running
2023-07-01 12:00:30,777 - root - ERROR - Timeout occurred
    </initial_app_log_contents>

    <!-- Correct counts derived from the log above -->
    <expected_debug_count>3</expected_debug_count>
    <expected_error_count>2</expected_error_count>

    <!-- Final state that the checker will enforce -->
    <final_service_conf_required_lines>
log_level = DEBUG
log_file  = /home/user/devops-demo/logs/app.log
    </final_service_conf_required_lines>

    <summary_file_path>/home/user/devops-demo/logs/debug_summary.log</summary_file_path>
    <summary_file_expected_contents>
DEBUG LOG SUMMARY
DEBUG=3
ERROR=2
    </summary_file_expected_contents>
